title: ischaemum mangaluricum
intro: Ischaemum mangaluricum, also known as the Bristle Club Grass, is a species of grass native to the tropical regions of India. Traditionally, it is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and soothing digestive issues. Ischaemum mangaluricum is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
